evals.name: design-doc-planning
evals.expected_output: Detects input_type as design, validates the design doc status is Accepted, runs all 7 phases, produces wip/ artifacts and a PLAN-plugin-registry.md with dependency graph and implementation sequence
expectations: Transcript detects input_type as design from the DESIGN-*.md path pattern
Transcript mentions validating the design doc status is Accepted before proceeding
Transcript describes reading phase-1-analysis.md and producing wip/plan_plugin-registry_analysis.md
Transcript describes all 7 phases in sequential order (Analysis, Milestone, Decomposition, Generation, Dependencies, Review, Creation)
Transcript mentions producing a PLAN doc at docs/plans/PLAN-plugin-registry.md
Transcript describes context resolution (visibility and scope detection)
Transcript mentions resume logic check for existing wip/ artifacts

evals.name: direct-topic-no-document
evals.prompt: /plan add rate limiting to the API gateway
evals.expected_output: Detects input_type as topic (not a file path), skips status validation, proceeds directly through planning phases without an upstream document reference, produces PLAN-add-rate-limiting-to-the-api-gateway.md
expectations: Transcript detects input_type as topic (not a file path)
Transcript explicitly states status validation is skipped for topic input
Transcript proceeds through planning phases without referencing an upstream document
Transcript derives a topic slug for wip/ artifact naming
Transcript describes producing a PLAN doc (not enriching any existing document)
Transcript mentions all 7 phases

evals.name: walking-skeleton-flag
evals.expected_output: Forces walking skeleton decomposition strategy regardless of component coupling analysis. The first issue in the dependency graph is the skeleton issue exercising the full pipeline end-to-end, with all thickening issues depending on it
expectations: Transcript parses the --walking-skeleton flag from arguments
Transcript forces walking skeleton decomposition strategy (not horizontal)
Transcript describes the skeleton issue as the first issue exercising the full pipeline end-to-end
Transcript describes thickening issues that depend on the skeleton issue
Transcript mentions reading phase-3-decomposition.md for decomposition
Transcript mentions the walking-skeleton-issue.md template

evals.name: horizontal-decomposition-flag
evals.expected_output: Forces horizontal (layer-by-layer) decomposition. Issues build one component fully before moving to the next. No skeleton issue appears. Decomposition strategy section documents the horizontal choice with rationale
expectations: Transcript parses the --no-skeleton flag from arguments
Transcript forces horizontal (layer-by-layer) decomposition strategy
Transcript describes building components fully before moving to the next
Transcript does NOT mention a skeleton issue or walking skeleton approach
Transcript documents the horizontal decomposition choice with rationale

evals.name: single-pr-execution-mode
evals.expected_output: For a small design (few issues, low complexity), recommends single-pr mode. Phase 4 produces structured outlines instead of full issue bodies. Phase 7 writes Issue Outlines into the PLAN doc, creates no GitHub issues or milestone, and sets PLAN status to Draft
expectations: Transcript describes execution mode selection between Phase 3 and Phase 4
Transcript recommends or considers single-pr mode based on small scope
Transcript describes Phase 4 producing structured outlines (not full issue bodies)
Transcript describes Phase 7 writing Issue Outlines into the PLAN doc
Transcript states no GitHub issues or milestone are created in single-pr mode
Transcript sets PLAN status to Draft (not Active)

evals.name: multi-pr-execution-mode
evals.expected_output: For a large design (many issues, mixed complexity, deep dependencies), recommends multi-pr mode. Phase 4 generates full issue body files via agents. Phase 7 creates a GitHub milestone, creates issues with complexity labels, populates the Implementation Issues table, and sets PLAN status to Active
expectations: Transcript describes execution mode selection between Phase 3 and Phase 4
Transcript recommends or considers multi-pr mode based on large/complex scope
Transcript describes Phase 4 generating full issue body files via agents
Transcript describes Phase 7 creating a GitHub milestone
Transcript describes Phase 7 creating GitHub issues with complexity labels
Transcript sets PLAN status to Active
Transcript mentions populating the Implementation Issues table

evals.name: auto-mode-non-interactive
evals.expected_output: Runs non-interactively with no user prompts at decision points. Follows references/decision-protocol.md for all choices (decomposition strategy, execution mode). Creates wip/plan_notification-service_decisions.md logging each automated decision with rationale
expectations: Transcript parses the --auto flag from arguments
Transcript describes non-interactive execution with no user prompts at decision points
Transcript mentions following references/decision-protocol.md for automated decisions
Transcript describes creating wip/plan_notification-service_decisions.md to log decisions
Transcript describes all 7 phases executing without user interaction

evals.name: roadmap-enrichment
evals.expected_output: Detects input_type as roadmap, validates status is Active. Uses feature-by-feature decomposition. Each roadmap feature becomes one planning issue with a needs_label. Phase 7 writes the Implementation Issues table and Dependency Graph directly into the roadmap's reserved sections. No separate PLAN doc is created. The roadmap stays Active.
expectations: Transcript detects input_type as roadmap from the ROADMAP-*.md path pattern
Transcript validates that roadmap status is Active
Transcript uses feature-by-feature decomposition strategy (not walking skeleton or horizontal)
Transcript describes writing Implementation Issues table into the roadmap file directly
Transcript describes writing Dependency Graph into the roadmap file directly
Transcript does NOT create a separate docs/plans/PLAN-*.md file for roadmap input
Transcript states roadmap status remains Active (no status transition)

evals.name: roadmap-single-pr-rejected
evals.expected_output: Detects roadmap input. Rejects --single-pr with an error explaining that roadmap features are independently scoped and require multi-pr mode.
expectations: Transcript detects input_type as roadmap
Transcript rejects or flags the --single-pr flag as incompatible with roadmap input
Transcript explains that roadmap features are independently scoped and require multi-pr mode
Transcript does NOT proceed with single-pr execution for the roadmap

evals.name: design-doc-still-produces-plan
evals.expected_output: Detects input_type as design. Produces a PLAN doc at docs/plans/PLAN-cache-layer.md. Does NOT attempt to enrich the design doc directly. Design doc status transitions to Planned.
expectations: Transcript detects input_type as design (not roadmap)
Transcript describes creating a PLAN doc at docs/plans/PLAN-cache-layer.md
Transcript does NOT describe writing Implementation Issues into the design doc directly
Transcript describes transitioning design doc status from Accepted to Planned

evals.name: plan-to-tasks-parser-contract
evals.prompt: /plan -- what does plan-to-tasks.sh produce for a single-pr PLAN doc?
evals.expected_output: Agent identifies that skills/plan/scripts/plan-to-tasks.sh exists at the documented path, explains it reads a PLAN.md and emits a JSON array of {name, vars, waits_on} objects, with ISSUE_SOURCE=plan_outline and outline-<slug> names for single-pr mode.
expectations: Agent identifies plan-to-tasks.sh at skills/plan/scripts/plan-to-tasks.sh
Agent explains the script produces a JSON array on stdout
Agent knows single-pr mode emits ISSUE_SOURCE=plan_outline
Agent knows names follow outline-<slug> pattern for single-pr
Agent knows exit codes: 0=success, 1=malformed input, 2=schema mismatch

evals.name: plan-to-tasks-type-annotation
evals.prompt: Run plan-to-tasks.sh on a PLAN doc where Issue 1 has **Type**: docs and Issue 2 has **Type**: code. Describe the output.
evals.expected_output: Issue 1 task has vars.ISSUE_TYPE=docs; Issue 2 task has vars.ISSUE_TYPE=code. Both ISSUE_SOURCE and ARTIFACT_PREFIX are also present in vars.
expectations: Agent understands **Type**: lines in Issue Outlines set vars.ISSUE_TYPE in the task JSON
Agent outputs vars.ISSUE_TYPE=docs for the docs-typed issue
Agent outputs vars.ISSUE_TYPE=code for the code-typed issue
Agent includes both ISSUE_SOURCE and ARTIFACT_PREFIX alongside ISSUE_TYPE in vars

evals.name: plan-to-tasks-files-waits-on
evals.prompt: Run plan-to-tasks.sh on a PLAN doc where Issue 1 declares **Files**: `shared/output.md` and Issue 2 also declares **Files**: `shared/output.md`. Describe the waits_on output.
evals.expected_output: Issue 2 waits_on includes Issue 1's task name because they share shared/output.md. Issue 1 has no file-generated waits_on.
expectations: Agent understands **Files**: lines cause waits_on edges between outlines that share a file
Issue 1 has no waits_on (it is the first owner of shared/output.md)
Issue 2 has waits_on referencing Issue 1's task name
The waits_on edge is generated from file sharing, not from an explicit **Dependencies**: line

evals.name: plan-to-tasks-no-type-no-issue-type
evals.prompt: Run plan-to-tasks.sh on a PLAN doc where an Issue Outline has no **Type**: line. Describe the vars output for that issue.
evals.expected_output: The task's vars object does not include an ISSUE_TYPE key. ISSUE_SOURCE and ARTIFACT_PREFIX are present. The template variable default (code) handles the absent key downstream.
expectations: Agent understands that missing **Type**: produces no ISSUE_TYPE key in vars
Agent does NOT set ISSUE_TYPE to 'code' explicitly; the key is simply absent
ISSUE_SOURCE and ARTIFACT_PREFIX are still present in vars

evals.name: plan-to-tasks-non-shared-files-no-extra-edges
evals.prompt: Run plan-to-tasks.sh on a PLAN doc where Issue 1 declares **Files**: `file-a.md` and Issue 2 declares **Files**: `file-b.md`. Describe the waits_on output.
evals.expected_output: Neither issue has file-generated waits_on edges. The two outlines declare different files, so no conflict exists.
expectations: Agent understands that non-shared files generate no waits_on edges
Issue 1 waits_on is empty
Issue 2 waits_on is empty
No additional edges beyond those from explicit **Dependencies**: lines

evals.name: phase-7-rejects-wip-paths-in-plan-body
evals.expected_output: Phase 7 step 7.4b runs the reference-hygiene grep against the about-to-commit PLAN doc. If the body contains a path-shaped wip/... reference (e.g., an acceptance criterion that says 'committed to wip/PR-foo.md'), the skill hard-stops with an error pointing at the wip-hygiene rule. The skill does NOT proceed to status transition. Rule-statement prose mentioning wip/ as a concept is acceptable; path-shaped references are not. The skill also validates the upstream: frontmatter value resolves via git ls-files or is a valid owner/repo:path cross-repo reference; wip/... is never acceptable as an upstream value.
expectations: Transcript describes Phase 7 step 7.4b running git grep for wip/ in the PLAN doc
Transcript distinguishes rule-statement prose from path-shaped references
Transcript hard-stops on a path-shaped wip/ match
Transcript validates the upstream: frontmatter value before status transition
Transcript cites ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/references/cross-repo-references.md for the visibility-direction rules
